news_summary: A remarkable rescue after eight days highlights resilience and hope amid the Venezuela earthquakes.
news_summary_fr: Un sauvetage remarquable, huit jours après les faits, met en lumière la résilience et l'espoir face aux tremblements de terre au Venezuela.
news_summary_es: Un rescate extraordinario tras ocho días pone de relieve la fortaleza y la esperanza en medio de los terremotos de Venezuela.

articles.title: Survivor pulled from rubble eight days after Venezuela earthquakes
articles.summary: Hernán Gil was trapped under a collapsed multi-storey car park.
articles.content:
Speaking at a press conference on Thursday, Rodríguez called the earthquakes "a natural tragedy on a scale we never imagined".

She rejected criticism that her government had reacted too slowly, saying that thousands of officials had been deployed after the quakes.

"We've done everything in our power, and we'll continue to do everything in our power and more," Rodríguez told journalists.

A Chilean firefighter had earlier described the operation to rescue Gil as "without doubt the most complex and technically difficult which I've had to tackle".

Allan Madrigal, a paramedic with the Costa Rican Red Cross, told journalists at the site that Gil had "emerged just perfect" from the ordeal.

Madrigal is the rescuer who heard Gil's faint cries for help emerging from the rubble on Sunday.

"It was an emotional moment," he recalled, explaining that at first he had not trusted his own ears and asked a colleague to confirm that he "wasn't just imagining it".

From that moment on, rescuers raced to try and dig the security guard out.

Gil had been on duty in a small concrete booth in the basement of the parking lot adjacent to the Galerias Playa Grande mall in Catia La Mar when the twin quakes struck.

It appears that the booth created a shell around him, protecting him from the 140 tonnes of rubble which collapsed around and on top of him.

"He has told us that he does not even have a crushed nail," another Costa Rican Red Cross worker said shortly before Gil was pulled from the rubble.

Gil had been given water and medics had attached him to an intravenous drip while teams from Venezuela, Chile, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Mexico, Portugal and the United States worked to free him.

Parts of the access ducts rescuers built to reach him collapsed several times, highlighting the dangers the work posed to the rescuers as well as Gil.

Overnight, the search teams were finally able to establish visual contact with the survivor.

In footage recorded by a small camera inserted into the rubble where Gil was trapped, a Chilean firefighter could be heard asking him to turn his head towards the camera.
articles.reasoning: The article reports a remarkable rescue of a survivor trapped under rubble for eight days after a major earthquake, demonstrating successful international humanitarian cooperation and hope in disaster response. The story has broad significance as it highlights life-saving rescue efforts benefiting society during a natural disaster.
articles.personality_title: Man rescued alive after eight days trapped in Venezuela earthquake rubble
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – A powerful earthquake hit Venezuela, causing buildings to collapse, including a multi-storey car park near the Galerias Playa Grande mall in Catia La Mar. Many people were trapped under the rubble.

**What happened** – Hernán Gil, a security guard, was trapped inside a small concrete booth in the car park’s basement when the earthquake struck. Rescuers heard his faint cries for help eight days later and worked carefully to free him. Teams from Venezuela, Chile, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Mexico, Portugal, and the United States joined the difficult rescue effort.

**Impact** – Gil survived under 140 tonnes of rubble without serious injury, protected by the concrete booth. The rescue was one of the most complex and dangerous operations firefighters and medics have faced. This successful rescue shows how teamwork and determination can save lives even in the toughest situations.

**What’s next step** – Gil will receive medical care and support to recover from his time trapped underground. Rescue teams will continue searching for others who might still be trapped and will review the operation to improve future disaster responses.

**One-sentence takeaway** – After eight days trapped under earthquake rubble, Hernán Gil’s rescue highlights the power of international teamwork and hope in disaster emergencies.
articles.personality_title_fr: Homme sauvé vivant après huit jours coincé sous les décombres d’un tremblement de terre au Venezuela
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Un puissant tremblement de terre a frappé le Venezuela, provoquant l’effondrement de bâtiments, dont un parking à plusieurs étages près du centre commercial Galerias Playa Grande à Catia La Mar. De nombreuses personnes sont restées coincées sous les décombres.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Hernán Gil, un agent de sécurité, était enfermé dans une petite cabine en béton au sous-sol du parking lorsque le tremblement de terre a eu lieu. Les sauveteurs ont entendu ses faibles appels à l’aide huit jours plus tard et ont travaillé avec précaution pour le libérer. Des équipes du Venezuela, du Chili, du Costa Rica, d’El Salvador, du Mexique, du Portugal et des États-Unis ont participé à cette opération difficile.

**Impact** – Gil a survécu sous 140 tonnes de décombres sans blessure grave, protégé par la cabine en béton. Le sauvetage a été l’une des opérations les plus complexes et dangereuses auxquelles les pompiers et les médecins aient fait face. Ce succès montre que le travail d’équipe et la détermination peuvent sauver des vies même dans les situations les plus difficiles.

**Prochaine étape** – Gil recevra des soins médicaux et un accompagnement pour se remettre de son temps passé sous terre. Les équipes de secours continueront à chercher d’autres personnes potentiellement coincées et analyseront l’opération pour améliorer la réponse aux futures catastrophes.

**Une phrase clé** – Après huit jours coincé sous les décombres d’un tremblement de terre, le sauvetage d’Hernán Gil montre la force du travail international et l’espoir face aux urgences de catastrophe.
articles.personality_title_es: Hombre rescatado con vida tras ocho días atrapado bajo escombros de terremoto en Venezuela
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Un fuerte terremoto sacudió Venezuela, causando el colapso de edificios, incluido un estacionamiento de varios pisos cerca del centro comercial Galerias Playa Grande en Catia La Mar. Muchas personas quedaron atrapadas bajo los escombros.

**Qué pasó** – Hernán Gil, un guardia de seguridad, quedó atrapado dentro de una pequeña cabina de concreto en el sótano del estacionamiento cuando ocurrió el terremoto. Los rescatistas escucharon sus débiles llamados de ayuda ocho días después y trabajaron cuidadosamente para liberarlo. Equipos de Venezuela, Chile, Costa Rica, El Salvador, México, Portugal y Estados Unidos participaron en este difícil rescate.

**Impacto** – Gil sobrevivió bajo 140 toneladas de escombros sin lesiones graves, protegido por la cabina de concreto. El rescate fue una de las operaciones más complejas y peligrosas enfrentadas por bomberos y médicos. Este éxito demuestra cómo el trabajo en equipo y la determinación pueden salvar vidas incluso en las situaciones más difíciles.

**Próximo paso** – Gil recibirá atención médica y apoyo para recuperarse de su tiempo atrapado bajo tierra. Los equipos de rescate continuarán buscando a otras personas que puedan estar atrapadas y revisarán la operación para mejorar las respuestas a desastres futuras.

**Una frase clave** – Tras ocho días atrapado bajo escombros de un terremoto, el rescate de Hernán Gil destaca el poder del trabajo internacional y la esperanza en emergencias de desastres.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed painting of a sturdy concrete security booth glowing softly amidst a chaotic pile of rubble, with a faint, warm light emanating from within symbolizing hope and survival, surrounded by silhouettes of rescuers delicately working together under dim natural colors, capturing the tension and triumph of an eight-day rescue after an earthquake.
